sourceReport.title: 2026 Q1 United States Snacks BBE Automated Report

caveats: The deck is a preserved source report and has not been promoted to official canonical product data.
Chart cache values and processed text-derived metric records are suitable for prototype validation, not final business publication.
Embedded workbook data should be source-owner reviewed before pilot use or automated deck replacement.

slides.title: Building Iconic Brands That Move Culture
Our Better Brand Equity (BBE) KPIs are validated to growth outcomes
And so our BBE KPIs feed directly into the GN Quality Growth Map
BBE classifies our brands into Kantar ‘BrandZ™ Typologies’ by indexing vs. category and vs. ~50k other brands
We group these ‘BrandZ™ Typologies’ into 3 size/‘life stage’ tiers:
Has brand improved significantly* on KPIs vs. last period?
Building Iconic Brands That Move Culture
How to read charts in the Automated Report: Dummy US example
Provocations as brands teams dig further into BBE & GN 5 steps data:
Provocations as brands teams dig further into BBE & GN 5 steps data:
3. DEMAND POWERA measure of consumer demandPredictive of volume shareValidated to financial success
…’Demand Power’ typically driven by Meaningful Salience
Where do I stand in the category?[Headline]
Am I building strong brands over time?[Headline]
4. PRICING POWER The ability of a brand to justify a price premiumBased purely on perceptionsBroad indicator of the price a brand’s equity can support
Pricing Power is calculated on the same 3 pillars, with different weights
STOP. NOTE: Pricing Power is just one piece of a larger complicated puzzle around pricing strategies
Am I building strong brands over time?[Headline]
5. SALIENT How easily the brand comes to mindSalience is great for growing volumeHowever, the majority of brands in the PepsiCo portfolio don’t have an issue with salience.  Salien
Where do I stand in the category?[Headline]
Am I building a Salient brand?[Headline]
